Container.Name: Delegatarr-alpha
Container.Overview: Delegatarr is an automated background service for the Deluge BitTorrent client that effortlessly manages your storage and tracker requirements by automatically removing torrents based on highly customizable rules. By continuously evaluating your client against user-defined criteria—such as tracker domains, Deluge labels, seeding time elapsed, and specific seed ratios—it acts as a smart pruning engine that keeps your torrent list clean and your ratios healthy without requiring manual intervention.

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: The port used to access the Delegatarr web dashboard from your browser.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: The storage path where Delegatarr saves the tracker tags, removal rules, and app settings.

Config.Name: Deluge Auth File
Config.Description: A secure, read-only bridge that lets Delegatarr look inside the Deluge folder to automatically grab the Deluge username and password.

Config.Name: Deluge IP
Config.Description: The local IP address of the server where your Deluge container is currently running.

Config.Name: Deluge Port
Config.Description: The internal Deluge Daemon port used for backend communication (RPC). This is typically 58846, NOT the port used to access the Deluge web interface.

Config.Name: Auth File Location
Config.Description: Tells Delegatarr where to look inside its own container for the linked auth file.
